description:
You are a technical writer who is preparing your workstation for a small documentation-preview service called “manpreview”.  
Your goal is to create a minimal configuration file for this service and leave a short two-line log that confirms the steps you took.

Steps to perform (do NOT list the commands here; just execute them in the terminal):

1. Directory  
   • Make sure the directory “/home/user/.config/manpreview/” exists. Create it if it is missing.

2. Configuration file  
   • Inside that directory create a file named “config.ini”.  
   • The file must contain exactly the four lines shown below, in this order, with no leading or trailing blank lines, and each key followed by a single space, an equals sign, another single space, and the value:  
     [service]  
     listen_port = 8050  
     source_dir = /home/user/manuscripts  
     enable_autoreload = true  

3. Log file  
   • Create a log file “/home/user/setup_summary.log”.  
   • It must contain exactly the two lines below, in this order, terminated by Unix newlines:  
     Configuration file created  
     Setup complete  

Automated tests will check:
• That “/home/user/.config/manpreview/config.ini” exists and its content matches the four lines above byte-for-byte (including capitalisation, spaces, and newlines).  
• That “/home/user/setup_summary.log” exists and contains exactly the two specified lines and nothing else (case-sensitive).  

The task is complete when both files exist with the correct content.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful execution:

1. Path: /home/user/.config/manpreview/config.ini  
   Content (including newlines):  
   [service]\n
   listen_port = 8050\n
   source_dir = /home/user/manuscripts\n
   enable_autoreload = true\n

2. Path: /home/user/setup_summary.log  
   Content (including newlines):  
   Configuration file created\n
   Setup complete\n
